name: The Money Vault Jewelry & Loan
description_short: The Money Vault Jewelry & Loan is a Cincinnati-area pawn shop founded in 1918, offering collateral-based loans, jewelry sales, and merchandise purchases with a 4.4-star rating from 180 reviews.
description_long:
The Money Vault Jewelry & Loan operates as a pawn shop in the Greater Cincinnati area, specifically located at 7764 Colerain Ave in Cincinnati, OH. The business traces its heritage to Kohn's, one of Lima's oldest continuously operating businesses founded in 1918, and has expanded to serve the Cincinnati community while maintaining that legacy.

The company offers traditional pawn shop services including collateral-based loans, jewelry buying and selling, and resale of general merchandise including electronics, watches, jewelry boxes, and consumer goods. They work with customers on pricing negotiations and claim to offer merchandise at discounted prices compared to retail. The business operates six days a week (Monday-Saturday, 10 a.m. to 5:30 p.m., closed Sundays) and maintains a claimed business profile.

The Money Vault distinguishes itself through customer service-focused reviews highlighting staff knowledge and fair dealing. Multiple reviewers praise specific staff members for transparency in pricing, going above expectations with customer service, and offering better prices than customers expected. The business has generated positive feedback about merchandise quality and the purchasing experience, with one reviewer specifically noting free food, drinks, and gifts during their visit.

However, the business carries significant risk reflected in negative reviews. One customer reported a serious dispute involving a missing watch box returned without its contents, leading to a police report and ongoing non-resolution. This suggests potential accountability and dispute resolution concerns. While most reviews are positive (146 five-star ratings), the presence of unresolved customer disputes and the collateral-based nature of pawn lending means customers should exercise caution and verify merchandise condition and documentation thoroughly before transactions.
pros: 4.4-star rating across 180 Google reviews with 146 five-star ratings
Staff specifically praised for transparency on pricing and willingness to negotiate fair prices
Long business history (founded 1918) suggests operational stability and community presence
Located in accessible area (7764 Colerain Ave, Cincinnati) with six-day weekly hours
Customers report quality merchandise and good deals on electronics and jewelry
Staff described as knowledgeable about products (electronics, gaming consoles, jewelry)
Business representative engages with customer feedback and maintains claimed profile status
cons: Unresolved dispute documented where customer reported missing watch box contents with no resolution despite police report
Lack of apparent dispute resolution process or accountability mechanism visible in reviews
Risk of merchandise condition disputes and documentation gaps in collateral transactions
Limited transparency on pawn loan terms, interest rates, and repayment requirements not mentioned on profile
Negative review suggests potential inventory accountability issues with high-value items
best_for: Customers seeking quick cash collateral loans against jewelry, watches, or electronics
Bargain shoppers looking for discounted used electronics, jewelry, and consumer goods
Those needing pawn loans with flexible pricing negotiations
Buyers comfortable with used merchandise in established retail pawn environments
diagnosis: The Money Vault is best for customers needing quick collateral-based loans or seeking discounted used merchandise, backed by generally positive service reviews. However, the documented unresolved dispute involving missing merchandise and lack of transparent dispute procedures present significant risk—customers should thoroughly document all items, condition, and contents before completing transactions, particularly for high-value jewelry and accessories.
meta_description: Cincinnati-area pawn shop founded in 1918 offering collateral-based loans, jewelry sales, and merchandise.
